meta.release_notes:
# Changelog
All notable changes to this model will be documented in this file.

## [2.0.0] - 2024-02-19
### Added
n/a

### Changed
- Update Collections to Set for stronger semantics
  - PositionsCollection -> PositionsSet
  - AllocatedPlannedProductionOutputCollection -> AllocatedPlannedProductionOutputSet
- lastUpdatedOnDateTime
  - move from position to AllocatedPlannedProductionOutput
  - use samm datatype instead of custom Characteristic
- estimatedTimeOfCompletion -> use samm datatype instead of custom Characteristic
- updated dependencies
  - io.catenax.shared.uuid
  - io.catenax.shared.quantity
  - io.catenax.shared.business_partner_number
- migrated samm version from 2.0.0 to 2.1.0
- updated materialGlobalAssetId description to point to part type twin
- make materialGlobalAssetId mandatory

### Removed
- Harmonization for Industry Core
  - materialNumberSupplier
  - materialNumberCustomer

## [1.0.0] - 2023-11-20
### Added
- initial model

nodes.label: PlannedProductionOutput
nodes.preferredName: Planned Production Output of a Supplier
nodes.description: This aspect represents the remaining and planned production outputs of a supplier allocated to a customer. An allocated planned production output is described by a quantity, the site of the supplier and the date with time at which a production of a certain material for a certain customer is planned to be finished. The allocated planned production outputs may be linked to customer order position they have been scheduled for.

nodes.label: positions
nodes.description: The positions refer to positions within an order of the given product (see material number supplier, materialNumber customer and material global Asset Id) for which the production outputs are transferred. The positions can be differentiated by the order position reference. If no order position reference is given, then there is only one position containing all planned production outputs of the product.

nodes.label: materialNumberCustomer
nodes.description: Material identifier as assigned by the customer. This material number identifies the material (as planned) in the customer's database. For standard parts (e.g. screws) for which a customer does not have an own number, the material number supplier should be set in this field, too.

nodes.label: materialGlobalAssetId
nodes.preferredName: Material Number UUID of the Customer's Twin
nodes.description: Material identifier used uniquely to identify the Material (e.g. PartAsPlanned).

nodes.label: materialNumberSupplier
nodes.description: Material identifier as assigned by the supplier. This material number identifies the material (as planned) in the supplier's database.

nodes.label: orderPositionReference
nodes.description: The order position reference contains information to identify a position within an order.

nodes.label: lastUpdatedOnDateTime
nodes.description: Date and time at which the position has been updated by the supplier's systems. Date and time are set according to the timezone.

nodes.label: allocatedPlannedProductionOutputs
nodes.description: Planned Production Outputs already allocated to the customer.

nodes.label: supplierOrderId
nodes.description: This is the order number on the supplier side that is given by e.g., the supplier's enterprise resource planning system.

nodes.label: customerOrderId
nodes.description: This is the order number on the customer side that can be given by e.g., the customer's enterprise resource planning system.

nodes.label: customerOrderPositionId
nodes.description: This is an identifier for the position of an order defined by the customer. It's not a technical identifier, such as an UUID. This identifier is used within the business context when talking about the position.

nodes.label: plannedProductionQuantity
nodes.description:
Quantity of a product that a supplier plans to produce on a certain date and time for a specific customer (that means that it will be allocated to a specific customer's "allocated item stock").
Quantities that have already been produced are put on stock or are considered for delivery. Therefore those quantities MUST NOT be considered in the allocated production output to build a consistent view on the supply situation.

nodes.label: productionSiteBpns
nodes.description: One planned and allocated production output has always one site. The site is identified by the BPNS.

nodes.label: estimatedTimeOfCompletion
nodes.description: Date and time on which the planned and allocated production output is estimated to be completed. Date and time are set according to a timezone.

nodes.label: PositionsCollection
nodes.description: If any production for the customer is planned, then there is at least one position. If the supplier does differentiate the planned production according to the order positions (an OrderPositionReference is set), then multiple positions can be given. Among the positions, there could be only one position without an OrderPositionReference to either report the planned production independently of orders or to report internal stock orders planned for the customer.

nodes.label: AllocatedPlannedProductionOutputCollection
nodes.preferredName: Planned and Allocated Production Output Collection
nodes.description: Collection of planned and allocated production outputs that may relate to different production locations and estimated time of completions.

nodes.label: MaterialNumberCharacteristic
nodes.description: The material number is a multi-character string, usually assigned by an ERP system.

nodes.label: OrderPositionReferenceCharacteristic
nodes.description: The order position reference includes reference numbers for the order and the position. As these information are meant for the customer, the customer reference numbers are mandatory.

nodes.label: DateTimeStamp
nodes.description: The Date with the timezone.

nodes.label: OrderIdCharacteristic
nodes.description: This is a proprietary order ID, generated by either the supplier side or customer side.

nodes.label: OrderPositionIdCharacteristic
nodes.description: The position ID is unique for an order and used within business context to refer to a position.

nodes.label: Position
nodes.description: The Position can be planned for production at several sites. A position may be anonymous or may reference a position within an order.

nodes.label: OrderPositionReference
nodes.description: Encapsulates the references to identify a position within an order.

nodes.label: AllocatedPlannedProductionOutput
nodes.description: Quantity, site of the supplier and date with time at which a production of a certain material for a certain customer is planned to be finished.

nodes.label: QuantityCharacteristic
nodes.description: Describes the quantity and the unit of measure with which the planned and allocated production output is measured.
